findings.description: No bounds checking on task_label list length or batch size could allow resource exhaustion attacks

findings.description: Task names in configuration could be influenced by untrusted input leading to unexpected adapter lookup
findings.recommendation: Validate task_names at initialization time against allowed characters/patterns

findings.description: Division by zero could occur if r becomes 0 after initialization
findings.recommendation: Add explicit check that r > 0 before division operation

findings.description: No temporary tensor cleanup could lead to memory accumulation over time
findings.recommendation: Consider removing temporary tensors or use in-place operations where possible

findings.description: Redundant I/O calls in __init__: Path.cwd() is called multiple times within path validation logic which increases latency during large-scale model instantiations.
findings.recommendation: Calculate `cwd` and `base_path` once at the class level or pass them as parameters if they vary per instance, reducing repeated OS calls.

findings.description: Heavy I/O operations in __init__ (exists, is_file, access) impact initialization time significantly in high-traffic applications where this config is re-instantiated.
findings.recommendation: Consider deferring expensive file existence checks or caching the path validity during the application startup phase rather than per-instance initialization.

findings.description: Deep nested loop in `_check_symlink_chain` checks every parent ancestor sequentially. For deeply nested directory structures, this operation is linear O(depth) and can be slow.
findings.recommendation: Use a set to track visited paths during traversal to prevent redundant checks or optimize with path manipulation to limit checks to MAX_SYMLINK_CHAIN_DEPTH (already set to 5, but logic can be optimized to stop earlier).

findings.description: Regex pattern `CONTROL_CHAR_PATTERN` is created at module load time. While efficient, re-compiling this or handling it as a constant could be optimized.
findings.recommendation: Use the `stringprep` module or compiled bytes patterns if multiple sanitizations are run frequently. Currently it is cached but could be static.

findings.description: Valori di soglia di sicurezza importanti (MAX_PATH_LENGTH, MAX_SYMLINK_DEPTH) sono hardcoded nel codice invece di essere gestiti tramite variabili d'ambiente o file di configurazione.
findings.recommendation: Spostare questi parametri in un file di configurazione (.env o settings.json) per permettere ai deploy di variare le policy di sicurezza senza modificare il codice sorgente.

findings.description: Importazione specifica del modello (Qwen2_5_VLConfig) limita l'agnosticismo del codice e la riutilizzabilità futura con altri modelli LLM.
findings.recommendation: Definire un'interfaccia astratta o un adapter per la configurazione del modello per evitare dipendenze troppo strette dall'implementazione di transformers.

findings.description: La configurazione del logger viene inizializzata all'interno del constructor della classe, creando un accoppiamento forte tra la logica di business e la gestione degli output del sistema.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare un 'Dependency Injection' per l'inizializzazione del logger o configurare il logging separatamente prima di istanziare l'oggetto, seguente al Best Practice 'Configuration Management'.

findings.description: Controllo ridondante delle traversal attacks: `trusted_base_dir` viene controllato sia in `_validate_inputs` che in `_resolve_trusted_base`.
findings.recommendation: Semplificare la validazione centralizzando la check su un unico punto univoco per evitare duplicazione di logica.

findings.description: Il logger scrive su sys.stdout direttamente, potendo sovrascrivere stdout se l'applicazione lo gestisce diversamente.
findings.recommendation: Passare il logger configurato tramite parametri costruttrici o file di configurazione per garantire che il log target sia controllabile dall'esterno.

findings.description: Lo calcolato di indexing inefficiente all'interno di un loop: la lista task_indices viene generata iterando su task_label per ogni task unico, creando una complessità algoritmica O(N*M), dove N è la dimensione del batch e M il numero di task unici.
findings.code_snippet: task_indices = [i for i, t in enumerate(task_label) if t == task]
findings.recommendation: Pre-calcolare un mapping indice->task all'inizio della funzione o allocare indici univoci staticamente in base alla task_label esterna al loop interno per evitare O(N*M).

findings.description: Creazione di tensori intermedi in un loop per ogni task: ad ogni iterazione vengono allocati task_x e task_lora_value, aumentando la pressione GC e il trasferimento di memoria.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare maschere booleanhe avanzate per filtrare x direttamente nella moltiplicazione o accumulare risultati in un tensore continuo prima della distribuzione.

findings.description: Assegnazione scalare in un loop Python: l'assegnazione elemento per elemento all'interno di un loop Python (lora_output[idx]) è lenta rispetto all'assegnazione vettoriale.
findings.recommendation: Sostituire con lora_output[boolean_mask] = task_lora_value per gruppi o usare torch.gather/advanced indexing per assegnare in blocco tutti i valori vinuti dall'elaborazione del task.

findings.description: Image loading uses synchronous requests.get() without batching or caching, causing sequential I/O bottlenecks
findings.recommendation: Implement parallel image loading with ThreadPoolExecutor or use aiohttp for async requests, add caching for repeated URLs

findings.description: Memory allocation in process_images for pixel_values padding creates unnecessary large tensors without reuse patterns

findings.description: L'uso di server_default='false' come stringa per una colonna Boolean è errato. Dovrebbe essere False come valore booleano Python o sa.text('false') per la sintassi corretta di SQLAlchemy.

findings.description: Source code or logic should not reside in a .cache folder. Runtime cache directories should be used for temporary data only, not for application logic or classes that may be imported/executed by the application.
findings.recommendation: Move this configuration file to the main source tree, e.g., .jina_onnx/configuration_jina_embeddings_v4.py or under src/.config/

findings.description: Magic numbers and constants (e.g., MAX_PATH_LENGTH, MAX_PROJECTOR_DIM) are hardcoded in the class definition. They should be defined in a central configuration file or environment variables per Best Practice 2.
findings.recommendation: Move these constants to a config module (e.g., config.py or settings.py) and load them from configuration or environment variables.

findings.description: Logger handlers are initialized inside __init__ of a configuration object and attached to the global logger. If multiple instances are created, this creates duplicate handlers on stdout, potentially causing performance issues or duplicate logs for global logging.
findings.recommendation: Use a lazy logging approach or return an empty logger configuration. Configure logging at the application level rather than inside class initialization.

findings.description: The class hardcodes a dependency on a specific HuggingFace model configuration class (Qwen2_5_VLConfig), rather than abstracting the embedding configuration logic to be model-agnostic.
findings.recommendation: Abstract the parent class into an interface or configuration factory to allow switching models without modifying source code.

findings.description: In `_initialize_logger`, `sys.stdout` is hard-coded. Using the default formatter is insufficient for multi-tenant or diverse environments; a custom format with more context (like severity/domain) is preferred as per Best Practice 4.
findings.recommendation: Increase log message detail to include domain tags if possible, or use a structured logging library (e.g., python-json-logger).

findings.description: Docstring states exports include 'Dataset' but Dataset is not in __all__ or imported
findings.recommendation: Remove Dataset from docstring if not exported, or add Dataset import/export to __init__.py

findings.description: Missing actual enforcement on Python version requirement - only logs warning but doesn't block execution or raise exception
findings.recommendation: Raise SystemExit or RuntimeError if Python version is not compatible, not just log a warning

findings.description: Metadata constants (__version__, __author__, __copyright__) not in __all__ but accessing them is unclear
findings.recommendation: Move these metadata constants OUTSIDE __all__ or clearly document their intended usage

findings.description: Import error handling has redundant error message - same message is both logged AND raised in exception
findings.code_snippet:
logger.error(
    "Failed to import Project from models.project. "
    "Ensure the models.project module exists and is properly configured. "
    f"Original error: {e}"
)  
raise ImportError(
    "Failed to import Project from models.project. "
    "Ensure the models.project module exists and is properly configured. "
    f"Original error: {e}"
)
findings.recommendation: Differentiate the messages - log one concise message, then raise with appropriate error details

findings.description: Check constraint recreation on large tables may cause significant blocking during schema migration

findings.description: La funzione drop_index richiede 2 argomenti posizionali o keyword Name, ma ne vengono passati 3. L'ultimo argomento (lista columns) non è supportato. Nel codice: op.drop_index('ix_policies_require_business_context', 'policies', ['require_business_context'])
findings.recommendation: Rimuovi i parametri separati per table e columns da drop_index. Usa op.drop_index('ix_policies_require_business_context'). L'indice è unico come nome, quindi l'indicizzazione può essere trovata.

findings.description: server_default='false' utilizza una stringa per impostare il valore booleano. Questo può portare a conflitti di tipo nell'applicazione perché SQLAlchemy BooleanType si aspetta un booleano o un valore SQL coerente. La funzione sa.false() è preferibile per sicurezza.
findings.recommendation: Sostituisci server_default='false' con server_default=sa.false() per garantire tipizzazione accurata e coerenza tra DB e Python.

findings.description: PostgreSQL-specific migration syntax used without database-agnostic fallback - CREATE INDEX with postgresql_where will fail on non-PostgreSQL databases
findings.recommendation: Use op.create_index() without database-specific parameters or wrap with try/except to handle dialect differences

findings.description: Hardcoded enum values 'direttiva', 'guardrail', 'suggerimento' in check constraint - violates configuration management best practice
findings.recommendation: Define these values as constants at module level or load from config file for easier maintenance and database-agnostic value definitions

findings.description: Hardcoded column default value 'direttiva' - should be configurable
findings.recommendation: Define default policy type as constant or environment variable for flexibility across environments

findings.description: Missing transaction quality check before migration - potential for leaving DB in inconsistent state if migration fails mid-way
findings.recommendation: Wrap all DDL operations in transaction and add SECTION markers to understand migration blocks

findings.description: No safety check before dropping index/constraint - will fail if these objects don't exist in older DBs
findings.recommendation: Use op.has_index() or try/except to safely handle optional migration operations in downgrade path

findings.description: Hardcoded specific tool names and LLM dependencies (e.g., 'llm_judge', 'web_search') violate Code Agnosticism and hinder future maintenance or model replacement.
findings.recommendation: Introduce an abstraction layer for tools/models. Use a configuration file or environment variable to define available tools and LLM providers instead of hardcoding them in the template definition.

findings.description: Magic numbers and hardcoded thresholds (e.g., min_depth=2, quality_threshold=0.8, min_sources=5) are defined directly in the code, violating Configuration Management best practices.
findings.recommendation: Move configuration values (depth, thresholds) to an external settings file or environment variables (e.g., .env or settings.yaml) and load them at application startup.

findings.description: Type hints generic 'Dict[str, Any]' without verification. The code assumes 'context' is a dict and calls dict-specific methods (e.g., 'get') without checking if context is actually a dict object.
findings.recommendation: Validate input parameters. Perform `if isinstance(context, dict):` check or ensure the `matches` method is only called with guaranteed types. Implement proper validation to prevent RuntimeErrors if `context` is None or another type.

findings.description: Large system prompts are hardcoded directly in the template strings (multiline strings in Phase.prompt_template). This makes maintenance difficult and embeds specific knowledge directly in the template logic.
findings.recommendation: Extract large prompts to external files or a configuration structure. This keeps the code logic focused and allows for easier localization, versioning, and updates without touching the template structure code.

findings.description: The `_` main block includes example usage, custom printing, and test data. This mixes template definition with application logic, potentially cluttering the module for other libraries or import context.
findings.recommendation: Separate the production code from examples. Move the `__main__` section to a dedicated test file (`test_01_..._test.py`) or documentation. Ideally, examples should be in a docstring or a dedicated `examples.py` file.

findings.description: Modifica della dir path (sys.path.insert) può permettere l'esecuzione di codice arbitrario se moduli malevoli sono caricati prima in precedenza, permettendo l'importing di pakketi non autorizzati.
findings.recommendation: Evita di modificare sys.path globalmente. Usa import statici o un logger_di_configuration specifico per il path.

findings.description: L'uso di 'import *' espone tutti i simboli del modulo al namespace, rendendo difficile l'audit dei simboli importati e portando potenzialmente di codice nel file origine che` modello database.contains

Utilizzare con cautela o avvolgere l'esecuzione in try-except per fallito, ma meglio isolare la porzione di codice in un blocco specifico per postgresql_only.

findings.description: Nomi delle constraint e indici sono hardcoded, il che riduce la Manutenibilità se si rinominano le colonne.

findings.description: Hardcoded policy_type enum values instead of using a constant or shared configuration. This creates duplication with application code and makes values maintain anywhere.
findings.recommendation: Create a constants module that defines valid policy_type values and reference it here for consistency

findings.description: upgrade() and downgrade() functions have similar structure with duplicate constraint operations. Consider parameterizing the values for easier maintenance.
findings.recommendation: Extract common constraint operations into helper function or use configuration to avoid code duplication

findings.description: The constraint parameter values are inline strings that are hard to maintain and update. No comments explaining the business logic of this check constraint.
findings.recommendation: Add comments explaining business rules and move the allowed values to a configuration source

findings.description: The constraint name 'ck_policies_policy_type' may not be descriptive enough about the actual business constraint.英文名可能不够明确。
findings.recommendation: Consider more descriptive constraint name or maintain documentation of constraint naming conventions

findings.description: Sequential import and type annotations are similar across both functions. Consistent formatting would improve readability.
findings.recommendation: Use consistent formatting and lint all Alembic migration files with the same style rules

findings.description: Missing metadata comment block at top explaining migration purpose, data impacted, and related changes.
findings.code_snippet:
"""add pillola to policy_type check constraint
Revision ID: a1b2c3d4e5f6
Revises: 0a70ee9d93ed
Create Date: 2026-03-04
"""
findings.recommendation: Add detailed docstring explaining what each migration function does and why the constraint was necessary

findings.description: No async/await usage - All I/O operations are blocking (file reading, API calls, subprocess calls). In production scale, this will block threads and reduce throughput.
findings.recommendation: Use httpx or aiohttp with async support for non-blocking API calls

findings.description: No caching for guidelines and best practices - These are re-read from disk on every single analysis call, wasting I/O.
findings.recommendation: Implement caching with TTL for frequently-read config files (use the config at load time once)

findings.description: Truncating large files with hardcoded 40,000 char limit - This wastes memory by loading entire file into memory before truncating, then creates a copy of the truncated content.
findings.recommendation: Stream file reading with character limit, or use mmap for large files to minimize memory usage

findings.description: os.walk processes directories synchronously without any parallelization - Could spawn multiple subprocess calls but threads are not used.
findings.code_snippet:
for root, dirs, files in os.walk(project_path):
    dirs[:] = [d for d in dirs if not d.startswith('.') and d not in exclude]
findings.recommendation: Use ThreadPoolExecutor or, better, async file system operations for concurrent traversal

findings.description: Multiple time.sleep() calls in retry loops block the entire thread - If running in a multi-worker server, these blocks prevent processing other requests.
findings.code_snippet:
if attempt < self.max_retries:
    self.logger.info(f"Retry {attempt + 1}/{self.max_retries} per {file_path}")
    time.sleep(2)
    continue
findings.recommendation: Use asyncio.wait_for with backoff, or async retry mechanism instead of blocking sleep

findings.description: Regex-based JSON parsing fallback is fragile and inefficient - Can cause multiple parsing attempts on failed responses, impacting response time.
findings.recommendation: Use proper JSON validation first, regex as last resort only with strict pattern matching

findings.description: Frequent subprocess spawning for linters blocks threads - Each subprocess creation is expensive and blocks the main thread.
findings.code_snippet:
result = subprocess.run(
    cmd,
    capture_output=True,
    text=True,
    timeout=30
)
findings.recommendion: Consider using aiohttp client calls to linters or pre-fork workers with process pools

findings.description: State file saved on every interaction (.save_state called multiple times) - Unnecessary disk I/O on every UI state change.
findings.recommendation: Debounce state saves or only save on configuration changes, not on every selection

findings.description: No progress tracking during long analysis run - If analysis takes minutes, there's no way to track remaining work or cache intermediate results.
findings.recommendation: Implement incremental progress reporting and consider caching analysis results per file per analysis type

findings.description: URL handling with urllib.request is blocking and doesn't support async operations, limiting server scalability.
findings.code_snippet:
req = urllib.request.Request(
    endpoint,
    data=body,
    headers={...},
    method="POST",
)
findings.recommendation: Use aiohttp or httpx with async/httpx module for non-blocking HTTP requests

findings.description: API key authentication bypass exists in production when WORKER_API_KEY is not configured
findings.recommendation: Always require API key authentication. Log a warning instead of allowing requests through without authentication in production mode

findings.description: Direct file system access to /proc/{pid}/environ for process environment inspection
findings.recommendation: Validate PID exists before accessing /proc filesystem and limit which PIDs can be inspected

findings.description: Uses unsafe subprocess commands with kill -9 on arbitrary PIDs
findings.recommendation: Implement proper authorization checks before killing processes. Log all process termination attempts with full context

findings.description: HuggingFace token loaded from .env file without proper validation
findings.recommendation: Validate token format before adding to environment. Consider using environment variables instead of .env file for tokens in production

findings.description: Potential SQL injection or command injection risk through model_id parameter in various lookup functions
findings.code_snippet: normalize = lookup.replace("vllm/", "", 1) if lookup.startswith("vllm/") else lookup
findings.recommendation: Implement proper input validation and sanitization for all external parameters. Use parameterized string matching instead of raw string operations

findings.description: No caching for expensive GPU and process queries - repeated subprocess calls increase latency and resource usage
findings.recommendation: Implement caching layer for GPU memory queries and process detection. Cache results for at least 30 seconds and invalidate on process changes

findings.description: N+1 query pattern when checking port availability in port allocation loop
findings.code_snippet:
for port in candidates:
    if not _check_port_active("127.0.0.1", port):
findings.recommendation: Batch port availability checks or maintain a cached list of known available ports to avoid repeated socket connections

findings.description: Multiple iteration loops for model config lookup could be undefined behavior with empty results
findings.recommendation: Fix typo - should return None instead of literal 'No'. This is a Python runtime error that will crash the application

findings.description: Hardcoded magic numbers throughout code (500, 2, 5, 3, 10, etc.) for timeouts and limits
findings.recommendation: Move timeout constants to settings/config files with descriptive names like TIMEOUT_GPU_QUERY, TIMEOUT_PROCESS_CHECK, etc.

findings.description: Functions exceed 50 lines or contain multiple responsibilities (God object pattern)
findings.recommendation: Split complex functions into smaller, single-responsibility functions. Extract subprocess execution, memory calculation, and tree traversal into separate functions

findings.description: Thread locks could cause deadlocks in multi-threaded environment
findings.recommendation: Consider using threading.RLock() or writing a reentrant lock wrapper. Avoid nested lock acquisition in the same thread

findings.description: Inconsistent naming: Mixed English and Italian comments/strings
findings.code_snippet: logger.info("ℹ️ [WORKER] Ignoro preload/sidecar autonomi del worker")
findings.recommendation: Standardize all comments and log messages to English for consistency and internationalization support

findings.description: Too many global module-level variables making code harder to test and reason about
findings.recommendation: Move state to classes or pass as parameters. Initialize via dependency injection rather than module-level initialization

findings.description: Missing type hints in many functions despite typing import
findings.recommendation: Add comprehensive type hints to all functions and methods for better type checking and auto-completion support

findings.description: User-controlled file content is passed directly to system prompt without sanitization. An attacker with access to modify analysis files can inject malicious instructions to override the security auditor's behavior.
findings.recommendation: Implement file content sanitization to strip or escape potentially dangerous prompt injection patterns like '{/=[{</|>{', '//', '/*', '---', '### SYSTEM PROMPT:'.

findings.description: API key defaults to 'EMPTY' fallback value which could expose system to unauthorized API calls. The api_key should never be placed in code or use weak defaults.
findings.recommendation: Remove the fallback to 'EMPTY'. Require mandatory API key configuration via environment variables or secure configuration management. Log to .env file instead.

findings.description: The api_base is constructed from user-controlled configuration without validation. If config is changed by untrusted input, could redirect requests to internal/untrusted endpoints.
findings.recommendation: Validate api_base format with strict regex and ensure it only points to allowed external domains. Implement SSRF prevention rules.

findings.description: File path is used directly in file read operations without any path traversal protection. Could read files outside intended scope.
findings.recommendation: Validate file_path against allowed root directory. Use Path.resolve() and check if resolved path starts with expected directory.

findings.type: Insufficient Error Handling Exposes Internal State
findings.description: Error messages return raw stack traces or internal details in some cases, potentially exposing file system structure and system information to attackers.
findings.recommendation: Create generic error messages. Never expose internal file paths or stack traces in user-facing responses.

findings.description: JSON parsing attempts first try to parse full response and falls back to regex matching. This could lead to parsing malicious or tampered JSON structures.
findings.recommendation: Use json.loads() with strict configuration (allow=False for singletons). Remove or restrict regex fallback to verified responses only.

findings.description: Debug/Warning logs include raw file content and API responses which could be stored by malicious users
findings.code_snippet: self.logger.warning(f"No valid JSON in model response: {content[:200]}")
findings.recommendation: Stop logging raw model responses. Log only counts and metadata, not the actual content.

findings.description: User input (analysis_type.prompt_template) is used in f-string without validation or sanitization of template source.
findings.recommendation: Validate template contains safe content. Implement template escaping or allowlist of permitted template syntax.

findings.description: Subprocess calls use user-controlled linter commands directly without validation. Could lead to command injection if attacker controls platform config.
findings.recommendation: Whitelist allowed linters and commands. Validate command name against allowed list. Do not use user input in shell commands.

findings.description: State files (audit_state.json, audit_best_practices.md) are saved without any access control verification or permissions management.
findings.recommendation: Implement file access controls. Validate file permissions after writing. Store sensitive state in secure paths only.

findings.description: L'API key è obbligatoria solo in modalità produzione. In sviluppo la validazione è mancante, permettendo accesso non autenticato.
findings.recommendation: Implementare autenticazione più robusta indipendentemente dall'ambiente, o rimuovere endpoint sensibili non protetti in sviluppo.

findings.description: Le violazioni API key logging potrebbero esporre IP degli attaccanti, permettendo l'analisi delle fonti di attacco
findings.code_snippet: logger.warning(f"❌ Richiesta senza API key da {request.remote_addr}")
findings.recommendation: Non loggare dettagli sensibili degli attaccanti. Loggare solo eventi aggregati.

findings.description: I comandi shell per subprocess.run contengono valori non validati. Se i parametri vengono da input esterni, rischio injection.
findings.recommendation: Convalidare tutti i parametri che provengono da input esterni. Usare subprocess con `shell=False` e validare PID.

findings.description: Debug logging di URL e errori da api_esteriori potrebbe esporre informazioni sensibili dell'architettura.
findings.recommendation: Rimuovere debug logs di URL esterni e errori dettagliati dalla produzione.

findings.description: I modello_id da input esterni (es. dalle richieste API) non vengono validati/sanizzati prima dell'uso in comandi shell.
findings.recommendation: Validare e sanitizzare qualsiasi input che finisce in comandi subprocess.

findings.description: Percorsi assoluti hardcodati (/app/config/models.json) lì esposti potrebbero essere vulnerabili in scenari multi-tenant.
findings.recommendation: Usare variabili d'ambient per percorsi sensibili invece di hardcoding.

findings.description: Il tempo di processamento del modello potrebbe essere esposto nei log di debug/verbose.
findings.code_snippet: logger.debug("Lettura token HuggingFace fallita da %s: %s", env_path, exc)
findings.recommendation: Rimuovere dettagli sensibili dai log di debug anche se internali.

findings.description: La funzione _build_instances_status espone dettagli internali sui processi vLLM che potrebbero essere usati per fingerprinting.
findings.code_snippet:
return {
    "instances": instances,
    "processes": procs,
    "has_active_instances": len(instances) > 0,
    "timestamp": time.time(),
    "vllm_available": True,
}
findings.recommendation: Limitare le informazioni esposte per guardare ai monitor.

findings.description: Hardcoded file paths break portability and violate Configuration Management best practices. Paths should be configurable via settings or environment variables.
findings.recommendation: Move to configuration settings like settings.py or use configure_path() method that can inject paths at runtime

findings.description: Hardcoded paths in list cause duplication across files and maintenance burden. Any path change requires editing multiple locations.
findings.recommendation: Centralize path configuration in a single settings module

findings.description: No logging implemented for debug/troubleshooting. Violates Error Handling best practice - need to log errors with context
findings.recommendation: Add proper logging with domain and level params, log the search path and result before raising exception

findings.description: Using runpy without graceful degradation for dependency failures. If the target script fails, no graceful degradation is implemented.
findings.recommendation: Wrap in try-except block with proper error handling and logging before deferring execution

findings.description: Missing docstrings on public function violates Code Quality best practices
findings.recommendation: Add docstring explaining function purpose, parameters, and return value

findings.description: Candidates list is hardcoded inline instead of being more maintainable with constants
findings.recommendation: Extract to a constant variable or configuration class for easier maintenance

findings.description: La funzione run_extraction non implementa alcun controllo di autenticazione o autorizzazione prima di elaborare i file, permettendo potenzialmente l'accesso non autorizzato all'estrazione dati.
findings.code_snippet: def run_extraction(file_path: str, pipeline: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
findings.recommendation: Implementare middleware di autenticazione e autorizzazione a livello API che verifichi i permessi prima di chiamare questa funzione.

findings.description: Il parametro 'pipeline' tramite non viene validato o sanificato prima dell'uso. Un attaccante potrebbe fornire nomi di pipeline malevoli.
findings.recommendation: Validare che 'pipeline' sia una delle pipeline autorizzate nel sistema, o utilizzare whitelist/blacklist di valori consentiti.

findings.description: Il percorso completo del progetto viene esposto nei log quando l'estrazione fallisce, potenzialmente rivelando informazioni sensibili sulla struttura del progetto.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare riferimenti relativi nei log invece del percorso completo, o tracciare solo informazioni essenziali per il debugging.

findings.description: La variabile di ambiente 'DOCLING_DEVICE' viene impostata hard-coded nel codice invece di essere configurata esternamente tramite file di configurazione.
findings.recommendation: Spostare questa configurazione in un file di configurazione (.env o settings.json) esterno per evitare hardcoding di dipendenze.

findings.description: Le eccezioni durante l'estrazione vengono gestite ma non registrano tutte le possibili throw stati, rischiando di perdere informazioni importanti per troubleshooting.
findings.recommendation: Implementare un try-except wrapper attorno a extractor.extract per catturare ed eccezioni non gestite e registrarle appropriatamente.

findings.description: Candidates list could benefit from a single config-based path instead of multiple hardcoded paths
findings.recommendation: Use environment variable or configuration file to define the script path, reducing code complexity and improving maintainability

findings.description: Script lacks async capabilities for async operations in the embedded target script
findings.recommendation: Ensure the target script supports async patterns where appropriate for better scalability

findings.description: Funzione 'run_extraction' è sincrona (sync). In un contesto worker para-GPU, l'uso di metodi async sarebbe preferibile per non bloccare il thread event loop durante le I/O operazioni sottofase della GPU o della rete.
findings.code_snippet: def run_extraction(file_path: str, pipeline: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
findings.recommendation: Ridefinire la funzione utilizzando il decoratore 'async def' e assicurarsi che tutte le dipendenze chiamate richiaminhino anche in modo asincrono dove possibile per mantenere il worker efficiente.

findings.description: Ogni chiamata a 'run_extraction' istanzia un nuovo estrattore tramite 'get_extractor'. Se gli estrattori coinvolgono carichi di stato pesante o compilazione, questa operazione dovrebbe occurring una sola volta e riutilizzata.
findings.recommendation: Implementare una istanziione globale o un caching a livello di sessione per reutilizzare gli oggetti estrattori adatti, specialmente se i pesi o il contesto GPU sono statici per una pipeline specifica.

findings.description: Logging in hot paths o rotture si possono avere un impatto sulle prestazioni. Se l'eccezione si verifica frequentemente, il logger.warning potrebbe rallentare il flusso di creazione.
findings.recommendation: Considerare l'uso di un handler di log più performante o disabilitare la cache di log se le chiamate falliscono spesso in modo deliberato per evitare saturazione dei buffer.

findings.description: Uso di 'os.environ.setdefault' per impostare il dispositivo `'cuda'` hardcoded all'avvio del modulo potrebbe forzare l'uso della GPU anche su macchine senza GPU supportata, portando potenziali risorse o crash nocivi.
findings.recommendation: Spostare questa configurazione in un file di ambiente esterno (es. .env o settings) per rispettare le condizioni dell'hardware senza hard codificare default aggressivi.

findings.description: Uso di runpy.run_path per caricare ed eseguire script Python direttamente dal filesystem. Se la directory del target è scrivibile dall'utente, permette l'Esecuzione Arbitraria di Codice (Arbitrary Code Execution).
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare un museo di verifi de integrità (checksum o signature digitale) dello script prima dell'esecuzione. Se possibile,.compile il codice o verificare che lo script non sia modificabile dal sistema operativo utente.

findings.description: I percorsi dei file di destinazione sono hardcodati nel codice invece di essere passati tramite configurazione esterna.
findings.recommendation: Verificare i percorsi da un file di configurazione esterno, variabili d'ambiente o swigasini di deployment per garantire la manutenibilità e permettere il management centralizzato delle percorsi.

findings.description: Paths hardcoded in code violate configuration management best practices. Should use environment variables or config files.
findings.recommendation: Move hardcoded paths to configuration file or use environment variables like path to scripts

findings.description: Multiple path traversals in candidates list create redundancy and potential logic errors. First path takes precedence without clear criteria.
findings.recommendation: Use a single canonical path configuration with validation instead of multiple candidate paths

findings.description: No logging when running nested scripts. Error context missing for debugging purposes.
findings.recommendation: Add logging before running candidates with context, run id, and script path being executed

findings.description: No graceful degradation when runpy fails. Exception silently swallowed except for final SystemExit.
findings.recommendation: Add try-except block to catch and log runpy errors, implement graceful degradation for dependencies

findings.description: No type hints on function parameters. Inline type annotations should be used.
findings.recommendation: Add type hints: candidates: list[Path]

findings.description: Missing input validation. The script path is hardcoded without validation that target app actually has permission.
findings.recommendation: Validate file permissions before attempting to run, add authorization check for running scripts

findings.description: Configuration hardcoded: DOCLING_DEVICE default is set to 'cuda' directly in code.
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded environment variable defaults from code. Configure device via environment variables or a configuration file at runtime.

findings.description: Missing exception handling for execution flow: extractor.extract() can raise exceptions during processing which are not captured.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the extraction logic in a try-except block to handle potential runtime exceptions gracefully. Log and return error instead of allowing process crash.

findings.description: Lack of specific type hints: Using generic Dict[str, Any] obscures data structure contract.
findings.code_snippet: def run_extraction(file_path: str, pipeline: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
findings.recommendation: Define specific return types (e.g., Optional[Dict] or custom dataclass) to improve type safety and autocompletion support.

findings.description: Paths to logging: Warning log message does not explicitly include a severity domain tag for easier filtering.
findings.recommendation: Adopt a structured logging approach (e.g., log domains) to allow better filtering and runtime monitoring of extraction failures.

findings.description: Missing input validation for dictionary structure. User inputs should be validated to prevent arbitrary data injection or malicious content.
findings.code_snippet:
if "prompt_token_ids" in prompt:
            return ParsedTokensPrompt(type="tokens", content=prompt)
findings.recommendation: Add schema validation to ensure dictionary only contains expected keys and valid types before processing.

findings.description: Error raised without logging context makes debugging difficult. Best practice requires logging with sufficient context.
findings.code_snippet: raise TypeError("inputs must be a string, TextPrompt, TokensPrompt, or EmbedsPrompt")
findings.recommendation: Log the error with details about the invalid input type and value to aid debugging.

findings.description: Type ignore comments may indicate incomplete type hints or compatibility workarounds.
findings.recommendation: Refactor to use proper type hints or update dependencies to resolve typing issues without workarounds.

findings.description: Prompt type priority logic (tokens > embeds > text) is documented inline but lacks formal documentation.
findings.code_snippet: # Keep token priority when both token ids and embeds are present.
findings.recommendation: Document this priority logic in the architecture folder as required by best practices.

findings.description: Multi-level nested if-statements for type checking could be simplified with early returns and explicit type guards
findings.code_snippet:
if isinstance(prompt, dict):
        if "prompt_token_ids" in prompt:
findings.recommendation: Consider flattening the type checking logic and using early returns for readability and maintainability

findings.description: Function is synchronous but is used in an async pipeline (vLLM). Consider making this function async-compatible if it becomes a performance bottleneck in high-throughput scenarios
findings.recommendation: Mark as `@async def` if it needs to support async calls, or add async cache layer for repeated non-changing inputs

findings.description: Repeated dictionary membership checks (`in prompt`) do not create memory overhead but repeated calls in tight loops could benefit from slightly more optimization
findings.code_snippet:
if "prompt_token_ids" in prompt:
            return ParsedTokensPrompt(type="tokens", content=prompt)
findings.recommendation: Consider building a type-dispatch pattern or caching the resolved prompt type if the same prompt is processed multiple times

findings.description: Hardcoded magic numbers for string slicing (200, 5, 300) should not be hardcoded in code
findings.recommendation: Replace magic numbers with named constants defined at module level with clear names like MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH = 200

findings.description: Project path manipulation using sys.path.insert() is fragile and non-portable
findings.recommendation: Use proper project structure with __init__.py or use explicit imports from project root instead of path manipulation

findings.description: Generic Exception handling swallows specific error types, making debugging harder
findings.recommendation: Catch specific exceptions like IntegrityError, SQLAlchemyError, or implement proper database-specific error handling

findings.description: Works with all policies at once without pagination - memory intensive for large datasets
findings.recommendation: Implement pagination or filtering to reduce memory usage, especially for large policy datasets

findings.description: No docstrings describing inputs/outputs/exceptions
findings.recommendation: Add proper docstring with parameters, return type, and exception documentation

findings.description: Generic traceback.print_exc() provided no context - each log should have domain and level
findings.recommendation: Implement logging with proper context: logging.error(f"DB_ERROR: {traceback.format_exc()}", extra={'domain': 'data', 'level': 'error'})

findings.description: Blocking keywords list should be externally configurable, not hardcoded
findings.recommendation: Move blocking_keywords to a configuration file or environment variable for maintainability and auditability

findings.description: Database connection not using context manager - DB session should be properly closed using 'with' statement to prevent connection leaks
findings.code_snippet:
db = SessionLocal()
try:
    # Recupera tutte le policy
    all_policies = db.query(Policy).all()
findings.recommendation: Use db.close() in finally block is done, but consider using Session() as context manager: 'db = SessionLocal(); try: ... except Exception: ... finally: db.close()'

findings.description: No exception type specification - catch all Exception instead of specific database/query exceptions
findings.code_snippet:
except Exception as e:
    print(f'❌ Errore: {e}', file=sys.stderr)
findings.recommendation: Use specific exception types for database operations (e.g., SQLAlchemy-specific exceptions)

findings.description: Inconsistent variable naming - using Italian names (guardrails, direttive, suggerimenti) instead of English per PEP 8 conventions
findings.code_snippet:
guardrails = [p for p in all_policies if p.policy_type == 'guardrail']
direttive = [p for p in all_policies if p.policy_type == 'direttiva']
suggerimenti = [p for p in all_policies if p.policy_type == 'suggerimento']
findings.recommendation: Use English variable names for consistency: 'guardrail_policies', 'directive_policies', 'suggestion_policies'

findings.description: Hardcoded magic number - arbitrary limit of 5 policies for display without explanation
findings.code_snippet: for d in direttive[:5]:  # Mostra solo le prime 5
findings.recommendation: Define a constant MAX_POLICIES_DISPLAY = 5 and use it consistently

findings.description: Print output instead of structured logging - violates logging best practices
findings.recommendation: Implement structured logging with domain and level parameters as per best practice #4

findings.description: List comprehensions inside functions without helper functions - should extract common CSV logic
findings.code_snippet: guardrails = [p for p in all_policies if p.policy_type == 'guardrail']
findings.recommendation: Create helper functions like 'get_policies_by_type(all_policies, policy_type)'

findings.description: sys.path manipulation at module import time is anti-pattern - project path should be added via configuration
findings.recommendation: Add project root to sys.path via configuration or import logic, not at module load time

findings.description: Default SECRET_KEY hardcoded as fallback value instead of requiring environment variable
findings.recommendation: Remove default value - always require FLASK_SECRET_KEY environment variable to be set. Consider raising an error if not provided.

findings.description: Model names and API keys mixed with configuration - should separate structurally
findings.recommendation: Create separate section for authentication/API credentials in settings files, not hardcoded in config module.

findings.description: Mixed language naming conventions in variable names
findings.recommendation: Consider using English naming conventions for variable/constant names across the entire codebase.

findings.description: Inline comments in Italian mixed with English in comments
findings.recommendation: Standardize code comments to one language (preferably English for documentation purposes) or move all Italian explanations to documentation files.

findings.description: BASE_LLM_CONFIG duplicated multiple times with modifications causing code repetition
findings.recommendation: Create a base template and use inheritance patterns or dynamic config building instead of copying and modifying the same dict.

findings.description: Prompts embedded inline instead of external file
findings.recommendation: Store prompt templates in external template files for easier maintenance and version control tracking of prompt changes.

findings.description: File is approaching 400+ lines - consider splitting into sub-modules
findings.recommendation: Create submodule config/models.py for model configs and config/settings.py for settings classes to improve maintainability.

findings.description: Lambda functions in dict may be harder to test and debug
findings.recommendation: Extract lambda functions into named functions for better testability and debugging.

findings.description: Hardcoded model paths should be configurable
findings.recommendation: Move all path configurations to config_manager or settings class for environment-dependent pathways.

findings.description: API key bypass vulnerability - if WORKER_API_KEY is not set (line 43-46), all requests pass through authentication check without verification
findings.recommendation: Block all requests if WORKER_API_KEY is not configured, regardless of environment mode. Add a fallback to logging only for development mode explicitly.

findings.description: SQL injection alternative - hardcoded URLs and port access patterns could be exploited through middleware bypass
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized requests and validate port ranges (0-65535) to prevent arbitrary port access

findings.description: Timing attack vulnerability - string comparison for API key uses == which is vulnerable to timing attacks
findings.recommendation: Use bcrypt for token comparison or use hmac.compare_digest for constant-time comparison

findings.description: Long function (_parse_vllm_cmdline) exceeds 100 lines, violates single responsibility principle (around line 335)
findings.recommendation: Split into helper functions for each concern: command parsing, environment reading, flag extraction

findings.description: Deep nested conditional in _parse_vllm_cmdline (6+ levels)
findings.code_snippet:
if not has_api_server and not has_vllm_serve and not has_transformers_embedding:
